message:
fix(schema): tighten command-shape map (jq notation, status alias, test hoist)

Code review pass on the new per-command shape map landed in a58633d3
turned up four small issues, all fixed here.

- **Notation inconsistency in `items_at`.** Originally used `"$"`
  (JSONPath) for top-level arrays but `".issues"` (jq) for the `list`
  command's wrapper. Now uses jq notation throughout — `"."` for "the
  top-level value is the array" and `".issues"` for "items live at
  `.issues`". Agents can compose `items_at` directly with `jq` without
  translating between path dialects.
- **Missing `status` alias entry.** `br status` is documented in the
  CLI as "alias for stats" and dispatches to the same handler, but the
  shape map only listed `stats`, so an agent looking up `commands.status`
  would get `null`. Now both keys point to identical envelopes — added
  a `stats_and_status_share_the_same_envelope` test to prevent the
  alias from drifting.
- **`build_schemas(All)` called per-loop-iteration in test.**
  `schema_for!` does real runtime work; rebuilding the catalog inside
  `command_shapes_have_consistent_invariants` once per command was
  wasteful. Hoisted out of the loop.
- **Inaccurate doc on `jq_filter`.** Said "for non-iterable outputs
  this is `.`" but `count`'s filter is `.count` (extracts scalar from
  wrapper). Rewrote the doc to describe the actual contract: extract
  the useful payload, where "useful" depends on the command's intent.

Also added an `items_at` invariant assertion (every value must start
with `.` so it's a valid jq path).

Verified: 6 schema tests pass, `cargo fmt --check` clean,
`br schema commands --json` round-trips cleanly with `jq` and the
`stats`/`status` entries are byte-identical.
